schools.summary: Gill: after the Levites were numbered and exchanged for the firstborn, they are numbered a second time, to take out such as were fit for service.

Henry: a second muster of the tribe of Levi — as the tribe was taken out of all Israel to be God's peculiar, so the middle-aged men were taken from among the rest to be actually employed in the service of the tabernacle.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron
a second muster of Levi
the able set apart for the holy service

schools.summary: Gill: they were to begin with the sons of Kohath and number them first.
Keil & Delitzsch: the sons of Kohath raised out of the sum total of the Levites, numbered first and specially, the men from thirty to fifty.

themes: Take the sum of the sons of Kohath... after their families
the Kohathites numbered first
theirs the most holy things to bear

schools.summary: Gill: thirty is the full strength of man and fifty the beginning of decline; the Levites set up and took down the tabernacle and bore its burdens on their shoulders from thirty upward; both John the Baptist and Christ himself entered on their public work at about thirty (Luke 3:23).

themes: From thirty years old... even until fifty years old
the prime of a man's strength
Christ beginning his ministry at thirty (Luke 3:23)

schools.summary: Gill: the service of Kohath concerned the most holy things. Calvin: God assigns the Levites their various offices, lest their promiscuous zeal beget confusion, and lest ambition stir some to put themselves forward — a rule prescribed, that none run about in aimless hurry.

themes: the service of the sons of Kohath... about the most holy things
each office assigned by God
order against confusion and ambition

schools.summary: Gill: when the camp set forward, upon the cloud's removing and the trumpets blowing, Aaron and his sons covered the ark.
Keil & Delitzsch: vv.5-15 — Aaron and his sons took down the covering veil and covered the ark of the testimony before the Kohathites bore it.

themes: when the camp setteth forward, Aaron shall come, and his sons
cover the ark of testimony
the holiest screened from every common eye

schools.summary: Gill: the badgers' skins were a covering made on purpose for the march, to preserve the ark from rain and dust, laid over the other coverings; the ark had three coverings.
Ellicott: the staves, removed while the ark was being covered, were then put back in their places (Exod. 25:15).

themes: a covering of badgers' skins... a cloth wholly of blue
three coverings for the ark
the holiest borne hidden yet honoured

schools.summary: Gill: the cloth of blue was spread that the table also might not be seen nor touched by the Levites.
Ellicott: the shewbread is called 'continual' because renewed every sabbath and continually before the LORD, even during the marches through the desert (Lev. 24:5).

themes: upon the table of shewbread they shall spread a cloth of blue
the continual bread shall be thereon
the fellowship of God and his people unbroken on the march

schools.summary: Gill: the scarlet cloth was spread upon the dishes, vessels, and the shewbread itself.
Keil & Delitzsch: the table, like the ark, was covered with a cloth and badgers' skins, and its staves put in for bearing.

themes: a cloth of scarlet, and cover the same with a covering of badgers' skins
the staves put in
the holy things in the hues of heaven and sacrifice

schools.summary: Gill: the candlestick had lamps continually lighted that gave light in the holy place; all its instruments — tongs, snuffdishes, oil vessels — were covered with the cloth of blue for the march.

themes: a cloth of blue, and cover the candlestick of the light, and his lamps
all the oil vessels thereof
the light of the sanctuary borne concealed yet unextinguished

schools.summary: Gill: the badgers' skins were here the outermost covering; the candlestick had only two coverings, being less sacred than the ark and table, which had three.
Keil & Delitzsch: the candlestick and all its vessels were put within a covering of badgers' skins and laid upon a bar.

themes: within a covering of badgers' skins... upon a bar
two coverings, not three
the ranks of sanctity kept in the carrying

schools.summary: Gill: the altar of incense was overlaid with gold, and so called the golden altar.
Ellicott: 'put in the staves thereof,' as with the ark and table.

themes: upon the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue
cover it with... badgers' skins... the staves
the place of intercession borne with Israel

schools.summary: Gill: these were all the instruments wherewith they ministered in the sanctuary, wrapped and borne.
Keil & Delitzsch: all the instruments of ministry put in a cloth of blue and covered with badgers' skins on a bar; then the altar of burnt offering was cleansed of its ashes.

themes: all the instruments of ministry... put them in a cloth of blue
cover them with... badgers' skins... on a bar
no vessel too small for reverence

schools.summary: Gill: the altar of burnt offering in the court, on which sacrifices were daily burnt, had its ashes removed (the more needful when the tabernacle was taken down), the fire doubtless preserved, and a purple cloth spread over it.

themes: they shall take away the ashes from the altar, and spread a purple cloth thereon
the altar fire preserved
the place of atonement borne onward

schools.summary: Gill: everything belonging to the altar was put upon it and covered with the cloth.
Keil & Delitzsch: the censers, fleshhooks, shovels, basons, all the vessels of the altar, were laid on it, covered with badgers' skins, and the staves put in.

themes: all the vessels thereof... the censers, the fleshhooks... the basons
a covering of badgers' skins... the staves
the full means of approach to God borne complete

schools.summary: Gill: 'the sanctuary' here means the holy things — the ark, table, and the rest — not the holy places; only after Aaron and his sons had covered them might the Kohathites come to bear.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Kohathites bore the holy things but were not to touch any holy thing, lest they die.

themes: the sons of Kohath shall come to bear it: but they shall not touch any holy thing, lest they die
the priests alone handling it uncovered
God sanctified in them that come near (2 Sam. 6:6-7)

schools.summary: Gill: to the office of Eleazar pertained the oil for the light, the sweet incense, the daily meat offering, and the anointing oil, with the oversight of all the tabernacle.

Keil & Delitzsch: the oversight of the oil for the candlestick (Exod. 27:20), the incense (Exod. 30:34), the continual meat offering (Exod. 29:40), and the anointing oil (Exod. 30:23) belonged to Eleazar.

themes: to the office of Eleazar... the oil for the light, and the sweet incense
the oversight of all the tabernacle
the costliest holy things in the most responsible hand

schools.summary: Gill: God gave Moses and Aaron a very solemn and awful charge about this affair, it being a hazardous one the Kohathites were employed in.
Keil & Delitzsch: a renewed command to prevent any calamity befalling the Levites while carrying the most holy things, lest through carelessness they be cut off.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron
the Kohathites' hazardous service
the priests charged to prevent calamity

schools.summary: Gill, with the Targum and Jarchi: 'cut ye not off' is 'give no occasion' — be not the cause of their cutting off through negligence or inadvertency in packing and delivering the holy things.
Keil & Delitzsch: the priests were not to cut off the Kohathites, but so to order the service that they might live and not die.

themes: Cut ye not off the tribe of the families of the Kohathites
give no occasion through negligence
the higher office bound to guard those beneath it

schools.summary: Gill: the directions that follow are the means of the Kohathites' preserving.
Wesley: each was appointed to that which was peculiarly allotted to him, the services and burdens being equally distributed among them.

themes: that they may live, and not die... appoint them every one to his service and to his burden
each man his definite charge
the burdens equally distributed

schools.summary: Gill: they were not to be present while the priests packed up, lest they see the holy things with their eyes, but to wait till all was covered; this may signify the hiding of the mysteries of grace under the former dispensation, when even the Levites were not admitted to a sight of them (cf. 2 Cor. 3:14).
Keil & Delitzsch: they were not to go in to see the holy things even for a moment ('like a swallowing'), lest they die.

themes: they shall not go in to see when the holy things are covered, lest they die
the look itself forbidden
the mysteries of grace hidden under the law (2 Cor. 3:14)

schools.summary: Gill: though Aaron is not named, he was concerned in taking the sum of the Gershonites as of the Kohathites.
Keil & Delitzsch: the service of the Gershonites is introduced in the same manner as that of the Kohathites.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses
the turn to the Gershonites
each Levite house ordered by one rule

schools.summary: Gill: though Gershon was Levi's eldest son, his posterity were not excused from the service of the tabernacle and bearing its things in journeying.
Keil & Delitzsch: take the sum of the sons of Gershon by the houses of their fathers, by their families.

themes: Take also the sum of the sons of Gershon
the eldest house not excused
no release from duty by birth

schools.summary: Gill and Keil & Delitzsch: the Gershonites numbered from thirty to fifty years old, all that entered in to perform the service in the tabernacle, the same rule as for the Kohathites.

themes: From thirty years old... until fifty years old shalt thou number them
the years of strength for service
one rule of age across the Levite houses

schools.summary: Gill, with Aben Ezra: 'to serve' of setting up the tabernacle, the shewbread, the sacrifices, and guarding it; 'burdens' of what they bore and carried in journeying — both a settled service and a charge for the march.

themes: This is the service of the families of the Gershonites, to serve, and for burdens
a charge for camp and for march
both ministry and burden

schools.summary: Gill: they bore the ten curtains of fine linen of the tabernacle.
Ellicott: distinguishes the mishkan (the dwelling of fine-twined linen curtains, regarded as constituting the tabernacle itself) from the ohel (the tent of goats' hair that covered it).

themes: they shall bear the curtains of the tabernacle... his covering... the badgers' skins
the soft fabric of God's house
each load exactly named and assigned

schools.summary: Gill: the hangings made the great court that surrounded the tabernacle.
Ellicott and Wesley: the curtains enclosed the whole of the court, which compassed both the tabernacle and the altar round about.

themes: the hangings of the court, and the hanging for the door of the gate
their cords, and all the instruments of their service
the boundary of the holy raised at every resting-place

schools.summary: Gill: all the service of the Gershonites was at the appointment of Aaron and his sons, as they should order and direct, every burden assigned by their charge.

themes: At the appointment of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service
ye shall appoint unto them in charge all their burdens
the service under settled priestly authority

schools.summary: Gill: this service the Gershonites should execute when the camp and tabernacle were in motion.
Keil & Delitzsch: their charge was under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron, carried out under his superintendence (cf. Exod. 38:21).

themes: their charge shall be under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest
a priest set over each Levite company
the chain of oversight in God's house

schools.summary: Gill: Merari, the third and youngest son of Levi, his house now numbered.
Keil & Delitzsch: the sons of Merari numbered after their families, by the house of their fathers, as the others.

themes: As for the sons of Merari, thou shalt number them after their families
the youngest house of Levi
the whole tribe drawn into the service

schools.summary: Gill: the Septuagint reads 'from twenty-five years old,' the age at which the Levites went into the tabernacle to wait and learn their business, though they did not enter fully upon it till thirty.
Keil & Delitzsch: from thirty years old to fifty, every one that entered into the service.

themes: From thirty years old... unto fifty years old shalt thou number them
entering at twenty-five to learn, serving fully at thirty
the service learned before the burden is borne

schools.summary: Gill: this was committed to the Merarites' care and charge, to bear and carry.
Ellicott: the Merarites carried the heavier and more cumbersome parts of the tabernacle and court, their specific duties assigned to each by name (v.32).

themes: the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars... the pillars... and sockets
the heavier and more cumbersome parts
the hidden framework on which all rested

schools.summary: Gill: the pillars of the court round about and their sockets among the Merarites' charge.
Keil & Delitzsch: 'all their instruments' — the things for setting up, fastening, and undoing the beams and bolts — reckoned to them by name.

themes: the pillars of the court... their pins, and their cords, with all their instruments
by name ye shall reckon
God's care that no part be lost

schools.summary: Gill: the families of Merari, the Mahlites and Mushites, with the service before described.
Keil & Delitzsch: the service of the sons of Merari, under the hand of Ithamar the son of Aaron the priest.

themes: This is the service of the families of the sons of Merari
under the hand of Ithamar
the houses ranked in ordered care under the priesthood

schools.summary: Gill: the chief of the congregation assisted Moses and Aaron in numbering the Kohathites.
Henry: of the Kohathites, 8,600 from a month old, only 2,750 were serviceable men, not a third part — of the many that add to the numbers of the church, few are serviceable.

themes: Moses and Aaron and the chief of the congregation numbered the sons of the Kohathites
only a third serviceable
membership is not ministry

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Kohathites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: From thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old
the serviceable men of the Kohathites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: the serviceable Kohathites numbered two thousand seven hundred and fifty.
Clarke: the full table — Kohathites 2,750 able of 8,600; Gershonites 2,630 of 7,500; Merarites 3,200 of 6,200; the whole Levites 22,300, of whom 8,580 were fit for service.

themes: two thousand seven hundred and fifty
the serviceable Kohathites of 8,600
God knowing the strength of his servants

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Kohathites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: These were they that were numbered of the families of the Kohathites
the serviceable men of the Kohathites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Gershonites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: And those that were numbered of the sons of Gershon
the serviceable men of the Gershonites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Gershonites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: From thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old
the serviceable men of the Gershonites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Gershonites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: Even those that were numbered of them
the serviceable men of the Gershonites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Gershonites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: These are they that were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon... whom Moses and Aaron did number according to the commandment of the LORD.
the serviceable men of the Gershonites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Merarites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: And those that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
the serviceable men of the Merarites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Merarites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: From thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old
the serviceable men of the Merarites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: the serviceable Merarites numbered three thousand two hundred.

Wesley: the wisdom of Providence — whereas the Kohathites and Gershonites, with fewer and easier burdens, had but about a third fit for service, the Merarites, with more and heavier burdens, had above half fit.

themes: three thousand and two hundred
above half the Merarite house fit
God fitting the strength to the burden

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Merarites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: These be those that were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
the serviceable men of the Merarites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Levites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: All those that were numbered of the Levites
the serviceable men of the Levites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: records the numbering of the serviceable men of the Levites, from thirty to fifty years old, fit for the work of the tabernacle.

themes: From thirty years old and upward even unto fifty years old
the serviceable men of the Levites
God's service borne by able and appointed hands

schools.summary: Gill: the total of the serviceable Levites, eight thousand five hundred and eighty.
Ellicott: this number of men between thirty and fifty bears a just proportion to that of all the males from a month old and upward, 22,000.

themes: eight thousand and five hundred and fourscore
the serviceable men of all three houses
a known and ordered company for God's service

schools.summary: Gill: Aaron and the chief men assisted; the 'service of the ministry' or 'service of service' is interpreted by Jarchi of the singing with cymbals and harps, by Chaskuni of the taking down and setting up of the tabernacle; all done according to the commandment of the LORD.

themes: According to the commandment of the LORD they were numbered by the hand of Moses
every one according to his service, and... his burden
all the work reckoned alike as service to God
